text: Fill in the blanks about reproductive physiology.
questions: The SRY gene on the *Y* chromosome directs the indifferent gonad to develop into a *testis*.
The menstrual cycle has three phases: *menstrual/follicular* phase (days 1-14), *ovulation* (day 14), and the *luteal/secretory* phase (days 15-28).
LH surge triggers *ovulation*, and the ruptured follicle transforms into the *corpus luteum* which secretes progesterone.
Testosterone is produced by *Leydig/interstitial* cells under the influence of LH, while Sertoli cells support *spermatogenesis* under FSH stimulation.
The combined oral contraceptive pill prevents pregnancy primarily by inhibiting the *LH surge* and thereby preventing *ovulation*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
